title: Azure Logic Apps - Sentinel Playbook Patterns From Real-World Examples
description: Complete step-by-step playbook patterns: email notification, group change, user block, MDE isolation, watchlist management, SharePoint write, Teams notification.

pattern_1_incident_email_notification.name: Incident Email Notification
pattern_1_incident_email_notification.description: Send a detailed email to a security team when a Sentinel incident is created. Most fundamental playbook pattern.
step_sequence: 2. Select_Entities: Project relatedEntities into {Entity, Entity Type} pairs
3. Create_HTML_table_with_Entities: Table action, format HTML, from Select output
4. Compose_Incident_link: Compose action, HTML anchor tag with incidentUrl
5. Initialize_variables (optional): Initialize a string variable for defanged entities
6. Send_an_email_with_Incident_details: Office 365 V2 Mail action with full HTML body

variant_with_foreach.description: Some notification playbooks iterate over account entities and send one email per account (e.g., MFA-Send-basic-email.txt). Uses Foreach over Entities_-_Get_Accounts output.

pattern_2_aad_group_change.name: AAD Group Change Playbook (Conditional Policy Enforcement)
pattern_2_aad_group_change.description: Add a user to an AAD security group to apply a conditional access policy. Checks if the user is already in the group before adding.
simple_pattern_step_sequence: a. Send_an_email (notify security team of action being taken)
b. Add_user_to_group (add to conditional access policy group)
Optional: also send a notification email to the affected user

advanced_pattern_with_idempotency_check.description: Check if the user is already in the group before adding (Group-Change-AADUser-Incident-Modern-auth.txt pattern).
step_sequence: a. Get_user: Look up user by AadUserId
b. Get_groups_of_a_user_(V2): Get user's current group memberships
c. For_each_1 (group GUIDs): Check each group
- If group equals target group GUID: Set_variable ModernAuth_Applied = true

pattern_3_user_block.name: Block AAD User (Disable + Revoke Sessions + Add to Group)
pattern_3_user_block.description: Comprehensive user blocking: disable the account, revoke all sessions, and add to a restricted group.
step_sequence: e. Add_user_to_group_2 (optional) — add to a second group

pattern_4_mde_machine_isolation.name: MDE Machine Isolation
pattern_4_mde_machine_isolation.description: Isolate a machine in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint when suspicious activity is detected. Includes server/workstation branching.
step_sequence: b. Condition_1: Is it a workstation (not server)?
c. Condition (testing gate): If Stop == false:
4. Send_an_email_(V2)_2 — notify that machine was NOT isolated (servers not isolated automatically)
pattern_4_mde_machine_isolation.isolation_order: Restrict execution → Isolate → Collect package → Start investigation → Run AV scan → Send email

pattern_5_watchlist_add_or_create.name: Watchlist Add Or Create Pattern
pattern_5_watchlist_add_or_create.description: Check if a watchlist exists; if it does, add items to it; if it doesn't, create it from scratch with CSV data.

critical_runAfter.Watchlists_-_Get_a_watchlist_by_alias: Must run with runAfter: [Succeeded, Failed] — 404 is a 'failed' response but expected

pattern_6_watchlist_delete_item.name: Watchlist Item Delete Pattern
pattern_6_watchlist_delete_item.description: Remove specific items from an existing watchlist by user identifier.
step_sequence: - Watchlists_-_Delete_a_watchlist_item: DELETE with watchlist alias and user ID in path

pattern_7_sharepoint_file_write.name: SharePoint File Write Pattern
pattern_7_sharepoint_file_write.description: Write a log or data file to a SharePoint document library for each incident entity.
step_sequence: - Create_file: POST to SharePoint connector with text content and dynamic file name
notes: File name combines user ID, incident number, and timestamp for uniqueness
Content is a flat key=value string (common for SIEM integration files)
queryParametersSingleEncoded: true prevents double-encoding of the folder path and name query params

pattern_8_teams_adaptive_card.name: Teams Notification via Incoming Webhook
pattern_8_teams_adaptive_card.description: Post an Adaptive Card to a Teams channel using an Incoming Webhook URL. No connector needed.
step_sequence: b. Append_to_array_variable: append Compose output to EntitiesJSON
4. HTTP: POST adaptive card to Teams webhook URL
pattern_8_teams_adaptive_card.sequential_foreach_note: The entities foreach uses concurrency.repetitions: 1 to ensure entities are appended in order before building the card

pattern_9_alert_suppression.name: Alert Suppression Pattern
pattern_9_alert_suppression.description: Triggered by Sentinel alert trigger; removes the alert from its incident under specific conditions (time, day, alert name).
step_sequence: a. Alert_-_Get_incident: GET incident ARM ID for the alert
b. Condition: If (day == Friday AND alert contains 'SAMR' AND hour > 21)

dual_sentinel_connections.description: Many playbooks use two Sentinel connections: one (MSI) for the trigger registration, another (service account) for action API calls. This is because the trigger webhook registration and the action API calls may require different authentication contexts.

entity_extraction_first.description: Always extract entities as the first action after the trigger. Entities_-_Get_Accounts or Entities_-_Get_Hosts must run before any foreach that needs entity data.
entity_extraction_first.reason: The relatedEntities payload from the trigger is untyped. The entity extraction actions return typed, structured entity objects (Accounts, Hosts, etc.)

error_handling_watchlist_check.description: Watchlist GET must use runAfter: [Succeeded, Failed] on the condition that follows, because a 404 (not found) comes back as a Failed action — but that's the expected 'create' path.
error_handling_watchlist_check.pattern: GET watchlist → runAfter[Succeeded, Failed] → Condition on statusCode==200
